_drug_naming_rule: Generic drug names are ALLOWED in the Treatment section only, inside a clearly labelled 'Exam pharmacology reference (UKMLA/MRCGP)' bullet with guideline attribution (BTS/NICE/SIGN/BNF) and a local-formulary caveat. The forbidden_prescriptive_patterns above catch doses and frequencies (absolutely forbidden) and prescriptive verbs + doses (absolutely forbidden). Brand names are caught by the uk_spelling_violations list if US spellings, and should be flagged manually otherwise.

timing_consistency_check.note: Timing language in sections 4 (Time Course), 7 (Red Flags), and 13 (Complications) must use the same phrasing. Flag any section that uses 'after 72 hours' without a lower bound.

eating_disorders: electrolyte monitoring including phosphate and magnesium

_comment: Updated 2026-03 (v2) — replaced blanket drug-name prohibition with two-tier rule: doses/frequencies always forbidden; generic drug names allowed in Treatment 'Exam pharmacology reference' bullet only with guideline attribution. Added dose-only pattern and frequency patterns. See CLAUDE.md for full drug naming rule.
